objective: Architect sign-off on reconcile_harness — the single mode-exact harness writer with strip direction, config.json mode persistence, and the 20-pair transition-matrix test.
key_changes: Architecturally sound: on-disk harness becomes a pure function of new_mode, eliminating the old_mode-residue drift class (bug 2). Strictly stronger than the removed write_harness.
Blast radius contained to cli/src/init.rs; no core/src/config.rs changes. Target->Mode 1-to-1 bridge keeps config surface stable.
Destructive ops are safe: strip is sentinel-bounded and .mcp.json edit removes only mcpServers.grove; host content + sibling servers preserved verbatim (explicit test).
Test rigor: 20 ordered A->B pairs across all 5 modes; fixtures structured for T07 reuse; active_mode round-trip covered separately.
Dry-run safe: provisioned.is_empty() guard short-circuits before reconcile/save. Non-TTY guard widened to treat config.json mcp-llm as already-configured.
Deployment: version bump required at release; no migration — drifted projects self-heal via one grove init --as <current-mode>. No security scan needed.
Follow-up: T07 fixture reuse needs a visibility change (non-blocking); tidy reconcile_harness doc comment (drives from new_mode, not on-disk state).
All prior phases approved; 278 tests green, cargo clippy -D warnings clean, files end with newline.
